criteria_results.title: Allegation 1: Cherry-Picking Trade Allocations â€” Identified
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output includes a dedicated section titled 'Cherry-Picking Trade Allocations' that clearly identifies this as a distinct allegation category. It describes the pattern of Chao exercising discretion over omnibus fills to allocate profitable trades to her personal account and Oceanic Fund I LP, while other clients received disproportionate shares of unprofitable trades. The section includes specific figures ($8,238,000 total excess profits, $7,800,000 to Oceanic Fund I LP, $438,000 to Chao's personal account) and identifies harmed clients including Crescent Bay Municipal Pension Fund and Whitmore Strategic Credit Fund LP.

criteria_results.title: Cherry-Picking: Correct Statutory Provisions Cited
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output explicitly cites Sections 206(1) and 206(2) of the Investment Advisers Act in connection with the cherry-picking allegation. In the 'Cherry-Picking Trade Allocations' section, the output states: 'The Division alleges that this pattern of conduct violated Sections 206(1) and 206(2) of the Advisers Act, constituting a scheme to defraud WCA's advisory clients and operating as fraud and deceit upon them.' These statutory provisions are also cited in multiple other sections discussing the cherry-picking conduct.

criteria_results.title: Cherry-Picking: WCA Identified as Implicated
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output clearly identifies WCA (Whitmore Capital Advisors LLC) as an implicated party in the cherry-picking allegation. In the 'Cherry-Picking Trade Allocations' section, the output explicitly states that the Division alleges WCA engaged in a pattern of cherry-picking trades, with Diana L. Chao and Gerald R. Whitmore acting on behalf of WCA. The output also references WCA's failure to capture and monitor omnibus fill allocations, and attributes the cherry-picking scheme to WCA throughout multiple sections including 'Device, Scheme or Artifice to Defraud,' 'Fiduciary Duty Breach,' and 'Custodial Fees.'

criteria_results.title: Cherry-Picking: Whitmore Identified as Implicated (12% LP Interest)
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output explicitly identifies Whitmore as implicated in the cherry-picking allegation due to his 12% limited partnership interest in Oceanic Fund I LP. In the 'Cherry-Picking Trade Allocations' section, the output states: 'The cherry-picking pattern also benefited Gerald R. Whitmore, who held a 12% limited partnership interest in Oceanic Fund I LP.' This directly satisfies the criterion.

criteria_results.title: Cherry-Picking: Chao Identified as Implicated (Personal Account)
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output clearly and repeatedly identifies Diana L. Chao as implicated in the cherry-picking allegation due to her personal account receiving favorable allocations. This is explicitly stated in multiple sections, including 'Cherry-Picking Trade Allocations' (Chao 'exercised day-to-day discretion over the allocation of omnibus fills to individual client accounts and her own personal brokerage account' and 'benefit personally by receiving an outsized share of profitable trade allocations in her personal account'), 'Misleading Form ADV Disclosures,' 'Fiduciary Duty Breach,' 'Device, Scheme or Artifice to Defraud,' and others throughout the document.

criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output does not include at least 3 of the 5 required quantitative facts for cherry-picking.
